title.en: Disagreeing Gracefully: "You have a point, but my thoughts are a bit different."
title.ko: 그 말씀도 맞지만 제 생각은 조금 달라서요 (조심스럽게 반대하기)

description.en: Don't just say "No." Learn the "Yes, but..." technique in Korean to share your different opinion while keeping the atmosphere professional and respectful.
description.ko: 회의나 대화 중 상대방의 의견에 반대해야 할 때! 무조건 "아니요"라고 하기보다, 상대의 의견을 존중하며 내 생각을 전달하는 세련된 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Workplace. Discussion. Disagreement. Manners. Negotiation. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 진지하고 조금 걱정스러운 목소리로 바로 시작
content.text: Mina, I had a meeting today and I really disagreed with my manager's plan.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I wanted to say "I don't think that's a good idea," but the room was so quiet that I felt like I'd be the "bad guy" if I spoke up. How do Koreans handle this?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아! 정말 어려운 순간이죠! 한국에서도 회의 중에..., "아니요" 라고 바로 말하는 건 꽤 큰 용기가 필요해요.
content.translation: Ah, that's a really difficult moment! In Korea too, saying "No" directly during a meeting requires quite a lot of courage.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래서 우리는 긍정으로 시작해서 나의 의견으로 끝내는 화법을 써요. 바로! "그 말씀도 맞지만 제 생각은 조금 달라서요" 라고 말이죠.
content.translation: So we use a speech style that starts with 'affirmation' and ends with 'my opinion.' Like saying, "You have a point, but my thoughts are a bit different."

content.speaker: John
content.text: "그 말씀도 맞지만"? So I'm basically saying "You're right, but..."?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 상대방의 의견에도 일리가 있다는 걸 먼저 인정해 주는 거예요. 그러면 뒤에 나오는 반대 의견도 상대방이 훨씬 잘 받아들일 수 있거든요.
content.translation: Yes! You're acknowledging first that the other person's opinion has merit. Then, they can accept your opposing opinion much better.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 분위기를 깨지 않으면서 내 목소리를 내는 고급 토론 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn 'advanced discussion reactions' to voice your opinion without breaking the atmosphere!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그 말씀도 맞지만 제 생각은 조금 달라서요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: This sounds very calm and logical.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "틀렸다" 가 아니라..., "다르다" 라고 표현하는 게 핵심이에요.
content.translation: Right. The key is to express it as "different" rather than "wrong."

insight.title: 그 말씀도 맞지만
insight.description: 'What you said is also correct, but...'
insight.usage_tip: 말씀(Words/Honored) + 도(Also) + 맞지만(Is correct but). This phrase softens the blow of the upcoming disagreement.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to ask them to consider another point?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "이런 점도 고려해야 할 것 같아요" 라고 덧붙여 보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try adding "I think we should consider this point too."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 내 의견을 강요하는 게 아니라 함께 생각해보자고 제안하는 아주 세련된 리액션이에요.
content.translation: It's a very sophisticated reaction that suggests thinking about it together, rather than forcing your opinion.

insight.title: 이런 점도 고려해야 할 것 같아요
insight.description: 'I think we should also consider this point'
insight.usage_tip: 이런(This kind of) + 점(Point) + 도(Also) + 고려해야 할 것 같아요(Seems like we should consider). Using '~것 같아요' makes the suggestion sound less aggressive.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. Mina and I are discussing a new design for our project. Mina wants something very flashy, but I think a simple design is better.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 이번 디자인은 무조건 화려하고 눈에 띄어야 해요! 색깔도 아주 강하게 쓰는 게 어떨까요?
content.translation: John, this design must be flashy and eye-catching no matter what! How about using very strong colors?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 사람들의 시선을 끄는 게 중요하긴 하죠. 미나 씨 말씀도 맞지만 제 생각은 조금 달라서요.
content.translation: Ah, it is important to catch people's attention. Your point is also correct, Mina, but my thoughts are a bit different.

insight.title: 미나 씨 말씀도 맞지만
insight.usage_tip: Using the person's name makes the reaction feel even more personal and respectful.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래요? 존은 어떻게 생각하시는데요?
content.translation: Is that so? What do you think, John?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 사용자들이 오래 봐도 질리지 않게 심플한 디자인으로 가는 게 더 좋을 것 같아요. 이런 점도 한번 고려해 주시겠어요?
content.translation: I think it would be better to go with a simple design so users don't get tired of it even after looking at it for a long time. Could you please consider this point too?

insight.title: 고려해 주시겠어요?
insight.usage_tip: Asking it as a question gives the other person room to agree without losing face.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 음..., 오래 봐도 질리지 않는 디자인이라... 그것도 일리가 있네요! 다시 한번 생각해 볼게요.
content.translation: Hmm, a design that people don't get tired of... that also has a point! I'll think about it again.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, it’s amazing! By acknowledging her point first, she was actually open to hearing my different opinion.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 상대방의 체면을 세워주면서 내 실속을 챙기는 게 한국 사회생활의 진짜 기술이거든요.
content.translation: Right. Saving the other person's face while taking care of your own interests is the real skill of Korean social life.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "graceful disagreement" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그 말씀도 맞지만 제 생각은 조금 달라서요!
content.translation: You have a point, but my thoughts are a bit different!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이런 점도 같이 고려해야 할 것 같아요!
content.translation: I think we should also consider this point together!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 일리가 있는 말씀인데 이런 건 어떨까요?
content.translation: That's a valid point, but how about this?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 좋은 의견이지만 조금 걱정되는 부분도 있어서요!
content.translation: It's a good opinion, but I also have some concerning parts!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 다른 관점에서도 한번 생각해 볼까요?
content.translation: Shall we think about it from another perspective too?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 누군가와 의견이 다를 때, "아니요" 대신 "맞지만" 이라는 단어로 대화를 시작해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, when you have a different opinion from someone, try starting the conversation with "correct, but" instead of "No."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "그 말씀도 맞지만 제 생각은 조금 달라서요."
content.translation: You have a point, but my thoughts are a bit different.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 듣고 보니 당신의 말에도 일리가 있네요. (Now that I hear it, your words also have a point.)

vocabulary.meaning: To consider / To take into account
vocabulary.example: 비용 문제도 함께 고려해야 해요. (We should also take the cost issue into account.)
서로의 의견을 존중하는 태도가 필요해요. (An attitude of respecting each other's opinions is necessary.)
같은 문제라도 관점에 따라 답이 다를 수 있어요. (Even for the same problem, the answer can be different depending on the perspective.)
상대방을 설득하기 위해 구체적인 자료를 준비했어요. (I prepared concrete data to persuade the other person.)
한국 사회에서는 상대방의 체면을 세워주는 것이 중요해요. (In Korean society, it's important to save the other person's face.)
